macro_segments.title: Structural Overview: The Seven Perakim of Bava Batra and the Context of Perek Seven

micro_segments.title: Introduction and Daf Assignment: Starting Daf 103 after the Mishnah on Daf 102b
Overview of the Seven Perakim: Neighbors (1-2), Chazaka (3), Buying and Selling (4-6), and Perek Seven as Conclusion to Sales
Future Perakim Eight through Ten: Death, Inheritance, Bequests, and Wills as a Distinct Section

macro_segments.title: The Measurement of Beit Kor: Units of Land and Practical Dimensions with Examples

micro_segments.title: Definition of Beit Kor as Thirty Se'ah and Converting to Modern Measurements: Football Fields and Acres
Comparison of Se'ah to Mishkan Courtyard Dimensions: 5,000 Square Amot as Reference Point

macro_segments.title: Rocks and Crevices in Land Sales: Measuring Non-Tillable Spaces and the Rashbam's Dimension Requirements

micro_segments.title: Mishnah Introduction: Rocks Ten Tfachim High and Crevices Ten Tfachim Deep Not Counted in Land Area
Rashbam's Addition: Four Tfachim by Four Tfachim Width-Length Requirement as Private Domain for Shabbat
Conceptual Framework: Why Height and Depth Matter—Creating Separate Domains Within the Field
Student Questions: Ownership of the Rock, Rock as Property, and Rights to Resources Within Non-Counted Spaces

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Challenge: Why Sanctified Rocks Should Be Counted Despite Not Being Measured—The Redemption Rate Problem

micro_segments.title: Source Tannaim: Sanctifying a Field in the Jubilee Era and the Torah's Fixed Redemption Rate of Fifty Shekalim per Beit Kor
Rashbam's Reading: Beshaat Hayovel (at the Time of Jubilee) Instead of Bishnath Hayovel (During the Jubilee Year)
Gemara's Logical Challenge: If Rocks Are Sanctified Separately, Why Do They Follow Different Redemption Rules?
Rav Ukva bar Chama's Solution: Untillable Crevices Filled with Water Are Not Considered Part of the Sanctified Field

macro_segments.title: Expanding the Framework: Tillable versus Non-Tillable Land and the Role of Bumps in the Field

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Clarification: Small Bumps (Negligim and Shadrei D'area) Are Part of the Land; Large, Defined Spaces Are Separate Units
Application to Sale Context: Buyer's Expectation of Tillable Land versus Acceptance of Minor Imperfections
Student Discussion: Permafrost and Untillable Land—Does the Buyer Have a Complaint if Much Land Is Untillable?
Rav Papa's Principle: Even Tillable Mounds and Crevices Are Excluded from Sale Because Buyer Wants One Flat Parcel

macro_segments.title: Quantitative Limits on Untillable Space: The Four Kav Rule and Its Exceptions

micro_segments.title: Rav Yitzchak's Principle: Non-Tillable Spaces Up to Four Kav in a Beit Kor Are Tolerable (Approximately 1/45 of the Land)
Mathematical Calculation: Four Kav Out of 180 Kav Total Equals Approximately 2% Margin of Tolerance
Rav Ukva bar Chama's Refinement: Four Kav Must Be Concentrated in a Space of Five Kavin (Contiguity Requirement)
Rabbi Chiya bar Abba Quoting Rabbi Yochanan: Four Kav Acceptable If Spread Within the Majority (60%+) of the Field

macro_segments.title: Unresolved Tannaitic Questions: Shape, Density, and Configuration of Untillable Areas

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya's Query: If Most Untillable Spaces Are in a Small Area and Least in a Large Area, Is It Still Acceptable? (Teiku)
Rabbi Yirmiah's Scenarios: Circular, Linear, Horn-Shaped (Ox Horns), and Zigzag Configurations of Untillable Patches (All Teiku)

macro_segments.title: Boundary Edge Cases: Single Rocks, Rocks at Field Edges, and Subterranean Variations

micro_segments.title: Tannaitic Rule: A Solitary Rock (Sela Yechidi) or Rock Adjacent to Field Boundary (Samuch Lemeitzar) Is Not Measured with the Field
Rashbam's Interpretation: Rock at the Edge Is Not Incorporated Into the Field Because It Stands Alone, Even If Small
Tosafot's Alternative: A Rock with Its Own Name (Having Distinct Identity) Is Separate from the Field
Rav Pappa's Question: If There Is a Small Amount of Dirt Between Rock and Boundary (Musheach Afar Beintayim), Is It Incorporated? (Teiku)
Rav Ashi's Question: If Rock Widens Below or Above the Boundary Line—Rock Wider at Top, Narrower Below, or Vice Versa (All Teiku)

macro_segments.title: Measured Sale (Beit Kor Mida Bechevel): Exact Dimensions and Adjustment Thresholds

micro_segments.title: Mishnah Setup: Seller Promises Precise Measurement ('Beit Kor Mida Bechevel') Instead of Approximate Sale
Shortfall and Overage Rules: Any Deficit Reduces Price (Pichat Kol Shehu Yinakeh); Any Overage Requires Return or Payment
Approximate Sale (Hen Chaser, Hen Yeter): Buyer Accepts More or Less; Tolerance of One-Quarter Kav per Se'ah
Mathematical Application: One-Quarter Kav per Se'ah × 30 Se'ah = 7.5 Kav Tolerance for Entire Beit Kor

macro_segments.title: Overpayment Remedy: Money Repayment or Land Return Options Based on Usability

micro_segments.title: Mishnah Question: What Does the Seller Return to Buyer for Overage—Money (Ma'ot) or Land (Karka)?
Rashbam's Rationale: Default Is Money Because Seller Cannot Use Tiny Overage Parcels; Improves Seller's Position
Condition for Land Return: If Overage Reaches Nine Kavin (a Usable Minimum for Field Growth), Buyer Can Force Land Return
Minimum Usable Sizes: Field (Beis Tishah Kavin), Vegetable Garden (Beis Chatzi Kav), Rabbi Akiva's Quarter Kav Standard
Total Return Principle: Buyer Returns Not Just the Excess but the Entire Overage Amount If Within Tolerable Range

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Chiya bar Abba / Rabbi Yochanan
